Document:
# Database Migrations
This guide covers how to create Entity Framework Core migrations for OpenEdge 14.
## When You Need a Migration
- Adding a new database table - Adding a column to an existing table - Modifying a column (type, nullable, default) - Adding indexes or relationships
## Migration Files
EF Core requires [bold]two files[/bold] per migration: 1. [mono]TIMESTAMP_MigrationName.cs[/mono] — the migration logic (Up/Down methods) 2. [mono]TIMESTAMP_MigrationName.Designer.cs[/mono] — auto-generated snapshot
[bold]Important:[/bold] The Designer.cs file must exist. If you generate the main file manually, you must also create the Designer.cs. Missing this file causes the migration to fail silently.
## Creating a Migration
# From the project root dotnet ef migrations add MigrationName --project Content.Server
If EF tools are not installed:
dotnet tool install --global dotnet-ef
## Migration Structure
[bold]Migration file (Up method):[/bold]
protected override void Up(MigrationBuilder migrationBuilder) { migrationBuilder.AddColumn( name: "BaseStrength", table: "CharacterStats", nullable: false, defaultValue: 5); }
[bold]Down method (rollback):[/bold]
protected override void Down(MigrationBuilder migrationBuilder) { migrationBuilder.DropColumn( name: "BaseStrength", table: "CharacterStats"); }
## Known Issue
When creating migrations manually (without dotnet ef), the Designer.cs must be generated from the ModelSnapshot template. Missing this file causes a known error where the migration appears to exist but doesn't apply on a fresh database. See CLAUDE.md errors section for details.
## Content Under Construction
Full migration documentation with Designer.cs template coming soon.
